name: 10k Science Adaptive Homepage Cards
description: Homepage-ready content cards derived from the public 10k Knowledge Base. These cards are designed for adaptive presentation, RAG retrieval, and audience-specific content selection.

selection_guidance.summary: Use cards for short adaptive homepage modules and use chunks for detailed answers. Rank by known audience, then topic match, then priority. Use source_chunks and canonical_url for attribution and deeper follow-up.

cards.title: Try 10k Science With Your Class
cards.summary: Educators can start a free trial account for the 10k Science web platform, including the browser-based web viewer and Educator Dashboard.
cards.body: 10k Science supports web and VR science learning. The web platform gives educators a way to try immersive science experiences, AI-guided learning, spoken student responses, and dashboard-based formative assessment without requiring every student to use a headset.

cards.title: Web Viewer And Educator Dashboard
cards.summary: The 10k Science web platform combines the browser-based Viewer, AI Guides, spoken student responses, formative assessment, and the Educator Dashboard.
cards.body: Students can explore immersive 3D science environments in a browser while AI Guides prompt them to observe and explain. Teachers can use the Educator Dashboard to review student progress, verbal responses, AI-assisted scoring, and patterns in understanding.

cards.title: Science VR And Web Learning For Classrooms
cards.summary: 10k Science combines immersive 3D environments, real scientific data, NGSS-aligned learning design, AI Guides, formative assessment, and educator-facing insights for classroom science learning.
cards.body: The platform is built for science concepts that are hard to teach with text, diagrams, or video alone. Students can explore microscopic, molecular, environmental, and systems-level science in VR or on the web.

cards.title: AI-Guided Formative Assessment
cards.summary: AI Guides prompt students during immersive science experiences, listen to spoken responses, provide feedback, and surface student thinking through the Educator Dashboard.
cards.body: 10k Science is positioned as a formative assessment tool, not a high-stakes testing system. Students explain what they understand while exploring, and teachers can use dashboard insights to identify where students need support.

cards.title: NGSS-Aligned Immersive Science
cards.summary: 10k Science content is developed to align with the Next Generation Science Standards in partnership with the Lawrence Hall of Science.
cards.body: The platform supports NGSS-aligned learning by giving students scientific systems to explore and prompts that ask them to observe, compare, explain, and connect ideas across scale, cause and effect, systems, structure and function, and stability and change.

cards.title: A Growing Library Of Immersive Science Experiences
cards.summary: The 10k Science library includes immersive experiences for topics such as CRISPR, sickle cell disease, plant disease, food molecules, pain signaling, wildfire smoke, antibodies, echolocation, atomic orbitals, and chemistry.
cards.body: 10k Science is strongest for topics that are difficult to understand from text, diagrams, or video alone: systems across scale, molecular and cellular processes, cause-and-effect mechanisms, body systems, environmental systems, and biomedical or engineering interventions.

cards.title: VR Biology For Web And Meta Quest
cards.summary: 10k Science helps students explore biological structures and processes through immersive 3D environments built from real scientific data.
cards.body: Biology experiences connect molecular interactions, cell processes, tissue responses, organ systems, organisms, and environmental conditions. Students can move across body, tissue, cell, molecular, and environmental scales.

cards.title: Built Around Real Scientific Data
cards.summary: 10k Science experiences are built around real scientific data, research-based visualizations, and collaborations with scientists and educators.
cards.body: Dynamoid's approach turns scientific datasets, structures, models, and mechanisms into explorable visual experiences that can support learning, research communication, and science storytelling.

cards.title: Researcher Bootcamps And Creator Workflows
cards.summary: Dynamoid uses researcher bootcamps and creator workflows to help scientists turn real scientific data into immersive 3D visualizations.
cards.body: Bootcamps give researchers access to 10k Creator workflows, science communication guidance, visualization support, and expert feedback. Selected visualizations can be adapted into 10k Science learning experiences.

cards.title: Applications Across Education, Healthcare, And Scientific Visualization
cards.summary: 10k Science's immersive 3D approach can support K-12 education, higher education, medical marketing, and scientific or industrial visualization.
cards.body: The strongest fit is for topics where audiences need more than a passive explanation: real scientific systems that are invisible, abstract, spatial, dynamic, or difficult to connect across scales.

cards.title: Evidence Of Engagement And Learning Promise
cards.summary: 10k Science has shown strong engagement signals and evidence of promise for science learning in pilot and survey materials.
cards.body: Reported signals include a 137-student pilot, 22 of 28 students preferring VR over reading or video, +15% content knowledge gains, 78% student engagement, and an independent Stevens Institute pilot where over 92% of undergraduates said the experience enhanced their learning.

cards.title: From Powers Of Minus Ten To 10k Science
cards.summary: Dynamoid's work began with the idea that vast amounts of 3D scientific data already existed, but much of it was unused, unconnected, and difficult for most people to experience.
cards.body: Dynamoid set out to use that data to build a navigable model of the microverse. Powers of Minus Ten reached 700,000+ users and helped establish Dynamoid's focus on scientific scale, visualization, and interactive learning.

cards.title: Supported By NSF, NIH, And 500 Global
cards.summary: Dynamoid's work has received support from NSF, NIH/HHS SBIR awards, and 500 Global.
cards.body: This support reflects a long-running development arc: from Powers of Minus Ten to 10k Science, with a focus on immersive science visualization, real scientific data, AI-guided learning, and classroom-ready formative assessment.

cards.title: Pricing, Trial, And Access
cards.summary: Educators can start a free trial account for the 10k Science web platform. Current pricing, trials, subscriptions, free experiences, and organizational licensing are listed on the live pricing page.
cards.body: Use the free trial CTA when a visitor wants to try the web viewer and Educator Dashboard. For current pricing or purchasing details, send users to the 10k Science pricing page.

cards.title: Frequently Asked Questions
cards.summary: The 10k Science FAQ answers common questions about the platform, devices, classroom use, content library, AI Guides, formative assessment, NGSS alignment, evidence, and pricing.
cards.body: Use the FAQ when a visitor needs quick answers or when a RAG system needs a compact answer source for common product questions.
